item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not sure how I missed this for so long, as much as I enjoy the Xia, Sarkic, and Mekhane stuff. This is magically good writing, and fits so well with already-established lore. 2217, in particular, was in the back of my mind throughout much of the piece. Stellar work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
An excellent work.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's actually just a typo, remains of previous versions of the draft. Thanks for pointing it out! Fixed to just SCP-2847-3.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2847-3-A claims that SCP-2847-2 is the only remaining subject capable of triggering this effect, but it is unknown how true this statement is.

A minor snip here: we know -3 is (supposedly) Boyi, but who else is this -3-A? What bugs me is that this -3-A appears only in the footnotes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: wonder what would happen if 2711 was taken to 2847? care to tell?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, okay. I'm just reading TOO far in to it, haha. Definitely took that last bit wrong, and the Library mention fueled that. Still, fantastic article (and I have since gone through several other Xia skips).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No like, there is no such implications. The whole serpent and dragon thing is not relevant to the Serpent's Hand, just similar name is all.

And the serpent folked said "cultists of the Library" only because they kind of held grudges because of them. I mean, if your entire history is gone from the books and the library kinda of denied you entrance because you "never existed", it won't really be a happy experience :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this, and upvoted, but I'm personally not sure how to feel about the implication that the Serpent's Hand are followers of Yaldabaoth - unless I've missed something and this is further expanded upon in the greater Xia mythos.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very difficult to sum up exactly what I like about this, because to me, everything about this is just perfect! Lots of exposition on an anomalous civilization, lots of Sarkic-CotBG canon addition, good and well written characters, nice inclusion of Chinese, great oldish language style for SCP-2847-3… love it! Every singly bit and piece of it!

++1! 'cause it's doubleplusgood!

You've really managed to make the Xia Dynasty one of my new favourite SCP cultures. I'm looking forward to reading more of your work. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks :3

As for the tale, well… the only thing that comes to mind is SCP-2217? Which is not exactly a tale and doesn't really involve Sarkicism, but is about Foundation, GOC and CotBG working together to fight flesh monsters? Not sure if it's what you want but it's a good article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Loved it!!! Very Creative!!
+1

Question: Do you know any tales of the Foundation, GOC, and COTBG working together against Sarkicism? I could use the reading material.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, I get it. A little cheesy, but I still like the main thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The use of -K is inspired from 2317, cause I want to emphasize the threat of SCP-2847-K(eter), making it sort of stand out. I mean, it's not entirely necessary, but I don't think it's a big problem either.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I mean why does it progress like this: -0, -1, -2, -3, -K? I meant the letter K when I said 'divulges suddenly into letters'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I mean, the letters are not sudden? They're retrived from -3 after it agrees to cooperate? It's just a progression from interviews to futher info they got?

As for the numbers, well, I tried to make it as clear as possible. The needle isn't marked as -1 cause it's purposely hidden in the first part, also it wasn't discovered until later. But it is one of the primary source of the anomly, so it's just given the designation SCP-2847.

These shouldn't be hard to figure out, but if you think it's confusing and all, I suppose that's fair.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A lot is really confusing, tho. SCP-2847, SCP-2847-1 (?), SCP-2847-2, SCP-2847-3, SCP-2847-K? Why does it suddenly divulge into letters? Wouldn't the needle be -1?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks Conwell :3

I suppose that is a valid point and I'll surely take that into consideration next time when I continue this series.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this SCP.

I'm not that familiar with Chinese mythology, but the Xia mythos skips always put on a good show, and make the concepts interesting to me. Additionally, I like the characterization of the AI, and its use of deception for what it believes to be the greater good.

I would argue that the "False flag article, your real article is in another castle" bit is starting to wear out its welcome (not quite to the level of End of Article Exposition Note, but its getting there). Still, I find the narrative and potential foreshadowing more than make up for that.

+1. Good job Sunny.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well mainly because the thing is in a very delicate situation right now? If -2 were to be neutralized, -K will break free, and Foundation doesn't have a completely safe backup plan in case that happens. And -2 could just be very easily killed so Foundation doesn't want to draw any attention to it.

The fake version is possibly the "public version" on the Foundation database, which aims at misleading people into thinking the monster is already sealed safely in a thousand-meter deep iron lake, and is unlikely to break free just like it has been for thousands of years. The fake version undermines -2's role so that people won't notice and the interview gives just enough info to present yet another vague story.

And in a narrative sense, all the actual Sarkic/CotBG are emphasized in the second version. I was hoping to create a sense of, presenting the conventional vague myth story in the first version, and revealing a much detailed and grounded truth in the second one ._.

Still, it's fair that if you feel like it's repetitive, and thanks for reading.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the general Xia mythos, but this felt rather slow and repetitive. Why was the initial "fake" article necessary (either in-universe or as a narrative tool)? Similarly for the first few interviews where -3 obscures the truth - that all seemed to lead to re-treading a lot of the same ground, and I'm not sure the "building of trust" narrative justifies the verbiage.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, the -K thing is kind of inspired from 2317, but other than that this has nothing to do with the Scarlet King. The number is a mere coincidence.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
7 beryllium-bronze alloy nails of 6 cm in length are embedded in the subject's vertebra.

SCP-2847-K

SCP-2XX7-K

Seven Seals, Seven Rings, Seven Brides Thrones Beryllium-Bronze Alloy Nails for the Scarlet King.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really nice to come back to the site and find this waiting for me. I really like how this area of the mythos is being developed, and the article is very well written and interesting. Keep up the good work!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That is a good point, but I'd say it's not a big issue and what I currently have can work. I don't really want to further confuse the situation or make the readers think they have to read 2711 for this or something. Thanks for the suggestion, but I'll keep it as it is :3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Tbh I have this concept in mind way before I wrote 2711. Um I just kind of thought using modified 2711 here as the prison can tie into the myth more, and it's fitting… The core concept of 2711 should be the Monkey King's story while this one focuses on Yu the Great, they kind of go different directions? I get what you're saying, and hopefully this is enough to differentiate the two. Thanks tho :3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Regardless, you continue to improve and improve as a writer :) This is great.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is really cool, not to mention that's a really clever way to keep something imprisoned.

My only real concern (and this doesn't affect my upvote) is that this really feels more like a rewrite and expansion of 2711. I mean, having this here makes 2711 as it is pale in comparison. This has so much more history and depth, I wonder at the necessity of keeping 2711 as its own separate article. Just a thought.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really really love the Xia dynasty mythology stuff, +1'd so hard

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nah, it's not that, no worries Meta, pat pat. I asked you about a tale and it's still in my sandbox and I haven't really gotten around to revise it :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yay! Was this the one you wanted me to review and then I kind of dropped the ball? I've been crazy sick for weeks :/

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I didn't get to finish my review, so I'm going to finish it here:

The idea to make an object identical to 2711 except for one thing but not actually called an instance of 2711 in the description doesn't make very much sense. Saying that it appears to be a modified version of 2711 makes more sense, as there only seems to be one difference between the two. Just saying it's an alternate version seems kind of unclinical to me.

"An instance of SCP-2711 is held in SCP-2847-X's hand, and has the additional anomalous property of repairing all damage done to it yadda yadda" would be an example of what I mean.

item.title: Re: Okay time for me to bullshit a few lines about Yin-Yang dualism in the comment section
item.content:encoded: That's a rather beautiful way to look at this, thanks Dora XD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks :3

As for Xia Dynasty's methods, their worshipping of Both Mekhy and Yalda is kind of a leftover product from the Yellow Emperor uniting different tribes worshipping one or the other, as mentioned (a bit) in 2481. Generally speaking, they do believe Mekhy and Yalda made the humans, but are not really religious, more of a practical bunch. They have no desire in helping either god, especially nowadays, as they escape the ultimate end that is to be caused by the two.

Their true purpose well, is to revive their great dynasty and not be turned into clockwork machines or flesh beasts :P They do like CotBG a bit better tho, especially after the Yu incident.

item.title: Okay time for me to bullshit a few lines about Yin-Yang dualism in the comment section
item.content:encoded:
Yu the sage and savior-king, enlightenment over darkness, craftsman of the divine metal - Yang.

The Beast of the Lake, lurking beneath the surface, lurking in the water, the threat felt but not seen, cannot be seen - Yin.

These two threads contrast and oppose each other throughout the story, subtly but surely, woven into the very fabric of the narrative…

… until the climax, wherein they instead turn to be one and the same. A darker and more ambiguous take on the classic notion of "harmony of Yin and Yang".

And isn't taking something that's meant to be comforting and making it evil the most classic of horror techniques?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oooh the place of Xia Dynasty in Mekhanite/Yaldabaoth conflict has been fleshed out.

It's still pretty confusing though, as Xia Dynasty used both Sarkic and Broken God methods in achieving their goals, which left me thinking that perhaps their true purpose is a third one different from either side. Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really liked this in chat, and I'm glad to see it on the site! I don't know too much about Chinese mythology and legends, so seeing some of those in this SCP is really interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A new addition to my Xia Dynasty/snake people series :D

It's related to my two other skips, SCP-2481 and SCP-2711, but their knowledge is not necessarily required for reading this.

Great thanks to War, MrHat, Lex1nat0r, Cimmerian, Cetus, Conwell, Blackfluid, MrWrong, Athenodora, Adnol, LordStonefish, Bluemorpho, Saga, Vacuum, DrBob, Petrograd, Roget, TheAlgatress, WestyDude, Oboebandgeek99, tretter, Fantem, and all the others who reviewed my draft.
